full_prompt: Design a 1-meter span test section of the primary main wing spar of a next-generation carrier strike fighter airframe benchmark, sized to meet MIL-HDBK-516C Part V airframe structural criteria and FAR Part 25 where applicable. Mission profile: combat radius 550 nmi, internal payload 4000 lb (two 2000 lb class weapons), approach speed <= 140 knots at full-flap configuration for carrier landing, sustained maneuver 7.5 g at Mach 0.9 / 15000 ft, catapult launch end speed 160 knots (tow load applied at the nose gear), and arrested-hook tailhook deceleration equivalent to 3 g forward over 2 seconds. Basic aircraft MTOW 45000 lb, wing reference area 500 ft^2, aspect ratio 3.5, half-span 20 ft. Spar material: Ti-6Al-4V (Young's modulus 113.8 GPa, Poisson 0.342, yield 860 MPa, ultimate 950 MPa, density 4430 kg/m^3) machined channel with thickness-tapered flanges. The 1 m test section is taken from the 60% to 70% semi-span range where bending moment under the design maneuver is 620 kN-m and shear 95 kN. Load cases: (LC1) maneuver ultimate: design limit 7.5 g x ultimate FoS 1.5 = 11.25 g on the local section inertial + aero distributed load, applied as a bending moment of 930 kN-m and shear 143 kN at the inboard cut plane; (LC2) negative g maneuver: -3.0 g x 1.5 = -4.5 g; (LC3) arrested landing: 3 g forward over 2 s adds a torsional component equivalent to 45 kN-m at the spar; (LC4) catapult launch: 160 knot end-speed tow shock imposes 4 g longitudinal on the spar pickup bracket equivalent to 60 kN axial; (LC5) fatigue: 10,000 flight cycles at mission spectrum 0.5-1.0-0.5 g-load LEF. Pass/fail per FAR 25.303 + MIL-HDBK-516: (R1) ultimate strength: max von Mises across LC1-LC4 <= 633 MPa (ultimate 950 / 1.5), (R2) yield: LC1-LC4 limit loads (LC/1.5) do not produce permanent set; yield load equivalents keep stress <= 748 MPa (yield 860 / 1.15), (R3) max deflection at spar tip of the 1 m test section under LC1 <= 15 mm, (R4) fatigue: alternating stress at bolt holes under LC5 below 160 MPa (Ti-6Al-4V Kt = 3 notched endurance at 10^4 cycles), (R5) local panel buckling linear eigenvalue under LC1 compression flange >= 1.5. Deliverables: STEP AP242, load envelope table, FEA per case, buckling eigenvalue, fatigue summary, and mass per meter.
short_prompt:
Design a 1 m span test section of the primary main wing spar of a next-generation carrier strike fighter airframe, taken from the 60-70% semi-span range of a wing with half-span 20 ft, wing area 500 ft^2, aspect ratio 3.5, MTOW 45,000 lb. Material class: Ti-6Al-4V machined channel with thickness-tapered flanges. Qualify to FAR Part 25 + MIL-HDBK-516C Part V airframe criteria, with ultimate FoS 1.5 on limit loads (FAR 25.303).

Load cases (spar section inboard cut plane values).
- LC1 ultimate maneuver: bending moment 930 kN-m + shear 143 kN (7.5 g limit x 1.5 FoS).
- LC2 negative maneuver: -4.5 g (-3.0 g x 1.5).
- LC3 arrested landing: 45 kN-m torsion (3 g forward over 2 s).
- LC4 catapult launch: 60 kN axial at the spar pickup bracket (4 g longitudinal, 160 kt tow shock).
- LC5 fatigue: 10,000 flight cycles at mission LEF spectrum (0.5-1.0-0.5 g).

prompt.title: Carrier strike fighter wing spar section
prompt.brief: 1 m section of main wing spar for a carrier strike fighter airframe.
